# Dennie Ordynskyi

> Product designer with 14+ years designing and shipping clean, user-centered experiences across web, mobile and AI-powered products. Currently designing large-scale consumer experiences at DraftKings, and creator of the Numi nutrition app.

## About
- Name: Dennie Ordynskyi (Denys Ordynskyi)
- Role: Product Designer
- Location: United States · remote-friendly
- Currently: Product Designer at DraftKings; Founder & Designer of [Numi](https://getnumi.app) and [Tysha](https://tyshaapp.com)
- Focus: AI-powered products, intelligent UX, product strategy and discovery, mobile and web apps, design systems, rapid prototyping, human-AI interaction
- Languages: Ukrainian (native), English (professional), Russian (professional)
- Status: Open to new product design opportunities and select collaborations

## Selected work
- [Numi](https://getnumi.app): AI-powered nutrition tracker for iPhone — snap a photo to instantly log calories, macros and micronutrients, with a personalized "Metabolic Fingerprint", plus fasting, weight and hydration tracking. Dennie's own product, designed and built end-to-end.
- [Tysha](https://tyshaapp.com): White-noise iOS app for new parents ("white noise for the 3am shift") — real-time white/pink/brown noise mixing, saveable Rooms, a fading sleep timer, Siri control, fully offline with no ads or accounts. On the [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/tysha-baby-sleep-sounds/id6783016339). Dennie's own product, designed and built end-to-end.
- [Linc](https://linc.cloud): Cloud studio platform-as-a-service; founding designer who built the design system and end-to-end UX.
- [MyGoTrainer](https://imdennie.com/#work): iOS marketplace connecting clients, personal trainers and facilities; owned the full UI/UX.
- [Shipshape](https://imdennie.com/#work): Smart-home connected hardware; led product look-and-feel and packaging.
- [Chatbox](https://imdennie.com/#work): Customer messaging SaaS; where Dennie started as a product designer.
- [E-commerce work](https://imdennie.com/#work): Built retail experiences and design practices, scaling an agency into an e-commerce brand.

## Case studies
- [Numi case study](https://imdennie.com/case/numi.html): How Numi turns food logging into a single photo — problem, product thinking, personas, key UX/UI decisions, design system and outcomes for an AI nutrition app. Currently in beta.
- [Tysha case study](https://imdennie.com/case/tysha.html): Designing a baby-sleep white-noise app for the 3 a.m. shift — one-handed, offline, half-asleep-proof. Live on the App Store (5.0 rating).
- [Karta case study](https://imdennie.com/case/karta.html): One QR code for a whole restaurant — designing a two-sided ordering and payment platform (guest web app + owner back office) end-to-end.

## Writing
- [Blog](https://imdennie.com/blog/): Practical writing on design careers, AI products, and building apps solo
- [How to become a product designer in 2026](https://imdennie.com/blog/how-to-become-a-product-designer.html): Career guide from 14 years in the field
- [Shipping two iOS apps solo with AI](https://imdennie.com/blog/shipping-apps-solo-with-ai.html): What AI changed about a designer's job
- [Designing AI products](https://imdennie.com/blog/designing-ai-products.html): Six UX lessons from building the Numi AI nutrition app
